# SAS Logos — Ship With The Deck

The SAS Asset Management logo travels **with** every deck, so a slide never falls back to a text-only "SAS Asset Management" wordmark. Two colour variants plus a self-recolouring inline-SVG option are provided.

**Never render the brand as plain text.** If you cannot embed a logo for some reason, that is a defect to fix, not a slide to ship.

## The two variants

| Variant | File | Fills | Use on |
|---------|------|-------|--------|
| **Primary** | `references/assets/sasLogoPrimary.svg` | navy `#002244` wordmark + green `#69BE28` mark | **Light** slides (white / off-white / light mesh backgrounds) |
| **Reversed** | `references/assets/sasLogoReversed.svg` | white `#FFFFFF` wordmark + green `#69BE28` mark | **Dark** slides (navy `#002244`, navy grid, dark mode) |

The reversed variant is the primary with every `#002244` swapped to `#FFFFFF`; the green mark `#69BE28` is fixed in both. Source: SAS-AM Webflow CDN primary logo (`65349e886047abfdf8265bde_SAS-Logo-RGB-Colour-PrimaryAlt-Med 1.svg`), reversed variant derived by the navy→white swap.

## Usage guidance

- **Navy logo on light slides, white logo on dark slides.** Match the wordmark to the background so it reads with full contrast.
- Standard placement: top-left of the slide (`top:70px; left:150px` at 1920×1080), height ~52px; a smaller ~38px footer lock-up on closing slides.
- The green mark is the constant — it stays `#69BE28` on every background.
- Keep clear space around the lock-up; do not stretch, recolour the green, or add a white box behind the logo on dark slides (the reversed variant already has transparent ground).

## Method A — theme-swapped data-URIs (self-contained, no external files)

Embed both variants and show the right one per theme. Base64 data-URIs mean the logo survives with zero network and travels inside a single-file deck.

```html
<img class="logo logo-light" alt="SAS Asset Management" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,">
<img class="logo logo-dark"  alt="SAS Asset Management" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,">
```
```css
:root[data-theme="light"] .logo-dark  { display:none; }
:root[data-theme="dark"]  .logo-light { display:none; }
```

### Primary (navy) data-URI — for LIGHT backgrounds
```
data:image/svg+xml;base64,
```

### Reversed (white) data-URI — for DARK backgrounds
```
data:image/svg+xml;base64,
```

## Method B — the `currentColor` recolour trick (one inline SVG, flips itself)

Alternative to shipping two files: inline the SVG once, set every navy path fill to `currentColor`, keep the green mark on its own class, and flip the wordmark colour with CSS. One asset serves both light and dark slides.

```css
.logo { height:52px; width:auto; color:var(--sas-blue); }  /* navy wordmark on light slides */
.logo .grn { fill:var(--sas-green); }                       /* green mark — constant */
.navy .logo, .bg-grid-navy .logo, :root[data-theme="dark"] .logo { color:#fff; } /* white on dark */
```
```html
<svg class="logo" viewBox="0 0 371 53" role="img" aria-label="SAS Asset Management">
  <!-- wordmark paths: fill="currentColor"  •  the two arrow marks: the green one uses class="grn" -->
</svg>
```

Both methods are brand compliant and flat design safe. Prefer Method A when copying a whole logo verbatim; prefer Method B when you want a single asset that recolours automatically across mixed light/dark slides.
